audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Passover and cloud-guidance text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for wilderness Passover, delayed observance for corpse-unclean or distant traveler, sojourner inclusion, cloud/fire presence, and movement/rest by YHWH's command.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses in wilderness of Sinai, in first month of second year after they came out from land of Egypt, saying,
Children of Israel shall make Passover at its appointed time.
On fourteenth day of this month, between evenings, you shall make it at its appointed time; according to all its statutes and all its judgments you shall make it.
Moses spoke to children of Israel to make Passover.
They made Passover in first month, on fourteenth day of month between evenings, in wilderness of Sinai; according to all YHWH commanded Moses, so children of Israel did.
There were men who were unclean by dead soul/life of a human and could not make Passover on that day; they came near before Moses and Aaron on that day.
Those men said to him, We are unclean by dead soul/life of a human. Why should we be diminished, not bringing offering of YHWH at its appointed time among children of Israel?
Moses said to them, Stand and let me hear what YHWH commands concerning you.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel, saying: Any man among you or your generations who is unclean by dead soul/life or is on a distant journey, he shall still make Passover to YHWH.
In second month, on fourteenth day between evenings, they shall make it; with unleavened bread and bitter herbs they shall eat it.
They shall leave none of it until morning and shall not break a bone in it; according to every statute of Passover they shall make it.

But the man who is clean and is not on a journey and refrains from making Passover, that soul/life shall be cut off from its people, because offering of YHWH he did not bring at its appointed time; that man shall bear his sin/misalignment.

When a sojourner sojourns with you and makes Passover to YHWH, according to statute of Passover and according to its judgment so he shall do; one statute shall be for you, for sojourner and native of land.
On day dwelling-place was raised, cloud covered dwelling-place, tent of testimony; in evening it was over dwelling-place like appearance of fire until morning.
So it was continually: cloud covered it, and appearance of fire by night.
Whenever cloud was lifted from over tent, afterward children of Israel set out; and in place where cloud settled, there children of Israel camped.
At mouth/command of YHWH children of Israel set out, and at mouth/command of YHWH they camped; all days cloud settled over dwelling-place they camped.
When cloud prolonged over dwelling-place many days, children of Israel kept charge of YHWH and did not set out.
Sometimes cloud was few days over dwelling-place; at mouth/command of YHWH they camped, and at mouth/command of YHWH they set out.
Sometimes cloud was from evening until morning, and cloud lifted in morning and they set out; or by day and by night, when cloud lifted they set out.
Whether two days or a month or many days, when cloud prolonged over dwelling-place, settling upon it, children of Israel camped and did not set out; when it lifted they set out.
At mouth/command of YHWH they camped and at mouth/command of YHWH they set out; they kept charge of YHWH, at mouth/command of YHWH by hand of Moses.

translation_notes: The delayed Passover creates an accommodation rather than permanent exclusion for people made ritually unclean by caring for the dead or prevented by travel.
The affected men speak up, Moses pauses, and new instruction is sought—an important model of responsive law.
Sojourner and native share one Passover statute; this must not become xenophobic gatekeeping.
Cloud and fire guide both movement and waiting. Divine guidance includes staying put, not constant productivity.
'At the mouth of YHWH' is preserved as command/guidance language.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 9 to shame mourners, travelers, disabled people, or those unable to meet a sacred date. The chapter itself creates a second opportunity and treats responsive accommodation as covenant faithfulness.
